'''Daniel Fitzhenry''' (born 8 December 1979) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s. He played for the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. He primarily played on the {{rlp|wg}}.

== Playing career ==
Fitzhenry played [[Jersey Flegg]] for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and had spent a period playing with Limoux Rugby League in France before being injured in a car accident.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

After coming through the joint venture [[Wests Tigers]]' ranks with [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]], Fitzhenry made his début in the [[National Rugby League]] playing at {{rlp|ce}} in Round 14 of the [[2002 NRL season]] against the [[Penrith Panthers]] at Leichhardt Oval.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

Fitzhenry played at {{rlp|fb}} for several games in 2002. His versatility has seen him play off the bench, as {{rlp|hk}} and once as {{rlp|hb}} (2004 against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]]. Injuries to the club's first choice player [[Benji Marshall]] saw Fitzhenry used as a makeshift 5/8th at times.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

Fitzhenry was a member of [[Wests Tigers]] team that won the [[2005 NRL Grand Final]], defeating [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] 30–16. He scored a try in the 63rd minute to give Wests a 12-point lead. He also scored a try in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]] which the Tigers lost to Bradford.

Fitzhenry held the record for the most tries scored by a player at the Wests Tigers, with 43, before being surpassed by Benji Marshall in 2010.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

In 2010, Fitzhenry returned to the Wests Tigers after two seasons playing with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite news|publisher=ABC Grandstand|title=Liam Fulton, Daniel Fitzhenry return to Tigers|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/liam-fulton-daniel-fitzhenry-return-to-tigers/story-e6frexnr-1225787688219|access-date=22 May 2010|date=16 October 2009}}</ref> He played in 18 games during the season, but was often named as a reserve back, getting little time on the field.

At the end of the season, Fitzhenry retired from the NRL, signing a two-year contract to captain-coach Southcity in the [[Group 9 Rugby League|Group 9]] competition.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=ABC|title=Fitzhenry to join Southcity in Wagga|url=http://www.abc.net.au/local/audio/2010/10/29/3051474.htm?site=riverina&section=sport|access-date=26 November 2010|date=29 October 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |title=Dan Fitz the Bill|journal=Rugby League Week |issue=23 February 2011 |location=Sydney |last=Logue|first=Matt |page=48}}</ref> He won a premiership in his first season with the club.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Daily Advertiser|title=Wests Tigers sign Wagga's Tim Hurst|author=Pamela Whaley|url=http://www.dailyadvertiser.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/wests-tigers-sign-waggas-tim-hurst/2410612.aspx|access-date=12 January 2012|date=5 January 2012}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.dailyadvertiser.com.au/story/4149376/flashback-southcity-v-gundagai-2011-group-9-grand-final-photos/|title=FLASHBACK: Southcity v Gundagai, 2011 Group 9 grand final – Photos|date=9 September 2016|publisher=Daily Advertiser|access-date=2 May 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.footyalmanac.com.au/almanac-rugby-league-grand-finals-in-group-9-a-reporters-view-from-inside-the-fence/|title=Almanac Rugby League – Grand Finals in Group 9: a reporter's view from inside the fence|last=Hauser|first=Liam|date=26 March 2020|website=The Footy Almanac|access-date=2 May 2020}}</ref>

=== Career highlights ===
*''Junior Club:'' Dorrigo & Nambucca Heads
*''First Grade Debut:'' Round 14, Wests Tigers v Penrith at Leichhardt Oval, 15 June 2002
*''Career Highlight:'' Wests Tigers Leading Tryscorer; try in the 2005 NRL Grand Final
*''Premierships:''  2005 (West Tigers v [[North Queensland Cowboys]])
